title: Arduino input output device and 2x LED's
Part-Time Shopify Developer - Long Term Role
description:
We are Nature’s Farmer Sea, an Australian ecommerce brand in the health and wellness space, selling direct-to-consumer via Shopify. We are growing quickly and need a reliable developer to support our website, subscriptions, and technical integrations. We value long-term tea
mbers and provide consistent work for the right person.



We’re looking for a part-time Shopify Developer to support our team with ongoing website updates, bug fixes, and technical integrations. This is a long-term, flexible role (~10 hours per week) working closely with our marketing team.



What You’ll Do:

- Make updates to our Shopify store (sections, pages, UX improvements)

- Assist with custom code (Liquid, HTML, CSS, JavaScript)

- Support and troubleshoot ReCharge subscriptions

- Help implement and manage app integrations (Klaviyo, bundles, upsells, etc.)

- Fix bugs and technical issues quickly

- Support product launches and campaign rollouts

- Ensure site runs smoothly across desktop and mobile



What We’re Looking For:

- Strong Shopify experience (Liquid, HTML, CSS, JavaScript)

- Experience working with ReCharge or Shopify subscriptions

- Experience with Shopify apps and integrations

- High attention to detail

- Reliable and responsive communication

- Able to work independently and problem solve



Bonus Skills! 

- Experience with Klaviyo integrations

- Basic CRO understanding (what makes a page convert)

- Experience working with ecommerce brands



Hours & Setup:

- Approx. 10 hours per week

- Flexible working hours

- Tasks assigned weekly

- Ongoing long-term role

- Paid weekly upon invoice 



To apply, please include:

1. Links to Shopify stores you’ve worked on

2. What you specifically did for each store

3. Your experience with ReCharge or subscription applications

4. A short Loom video (2–3 minutes) reviewing our website: 
 - what would you improve technically or from a UX perspective?

5. Your hourly rate (AUD) and weekly availability



Important: 

To confirm you have read this job description, please include the words “Sea Moss” at the top of your application.

title: Senior AI-Native Software Developer / Rapid Builder (Full-Time or Contract)
description:
We are looking for a high-speed, highly independent software developer who can build projects on demand with little to no supervision.



You will work on:

• Polishing and extending existing web apps

• Adding new features to live systems

• Building full projects from scratch by reading a short project proposal

• Connecting systems to Go High Level (GHL) and automations



This is not a junior role. We need someone who understands systems, moves fast, and executes without constant explanations.



CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

• Build new web apps or features from scratch quickly

• Improve, refactor, and extend existing websites/apps

• Integrate APIs, automations, and AI services

• Connect platforms to Go High Level (GHL)

• Deploy, test, and ship production-ready features

• Work independently from project briefs or proposals



REQUIRED EXPERIENCE (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

You must be proficient with:

• Vercel

• Render

• Resend

• OpenAI

• Google Cloud Platform

• GitHub



You must also be familiar with or actively using:

• Lovable / Bolt

• Supabase

• Replit



AI-NATIVE TOOLING (VERY IMPORTANT)

You should already be comfortable using AI daily for development, including tools such as:

• GitHub Copilot

• Cursor IDE

• Claude (3.5 Sonnet or higher)

• ChatGPT (GPT-4 / GPT-4o)

• Replit Agent

• Google Gemini Code Assist



If you don’t already build faster because of AI, this role is not a fit.



HOW YOU MUST WORK

• Little to no supervision

• High ownership over outcomes

• Able to read a project proposal and execute immediately

• Comfortable making technical decisions

• Fast iteration and delivery

• Strong communication when needed, silent execution otherwise



EMPLOYMENT TYPE

• Full-Time or Contract (hourly/project-based)

• Long-term opportunity for the right person



COMPENSATION

• Competitive pay based on speed, quality, and output

• Performance-based increases over time



TO APPLY (IMPORTANT)

Only applicants who follow instructions will be considered.



Please send:

Short summary of your experience

Examples of projects you built or shipped

Which AI dev tools you actively use (daily)

Your availability and preferred rate



FINAL NOTE

If you need:

• Step-by-step instructions

• Heavy management

• Long explanations



This role is not for you.



If you are a fast, AI-native builder who ships, we want to work with you.

title: Accounts Payable (AP) Specialist
description:
The Company



Del Playa Group (DPG) is a U.S.-based professional services firm specializing in accounting, finance, and audit solutions for businesses and CPA firms. We help clients expand capacity, strengthen controls, and elevate performance by offering three pillars: fractional CFO advisory, top offshore talent, and transformative technology.



The Role



We are seeking a detail-oriented and highly organized Accounts Payable (AP) Specialist to support our U.S. operations. This role will be responsible for managing end-to-end accounts payable processes, purchase order workflows, expense reporting, and vendor compliance.



The ideal candidate is proactive, systems-savvy, and capable of managing high-volume transactions with accuracy and efficiency. You will play a key role in maintaining strong financial controls, ensuring timely payments, and supporting overall accounting operations.



Key Responsibilities:



Purchase Orders



•Review purchase order (PO) requests for completeness and accuracy

•Convert approved PO requests into purchase orders and invoices

•Ensure proper documentation and alignment with company policies



Accounts Payable

•Set up ACH batches in Intacct

•Process ACH payments through the bank

•Manage and monitor the shared “Accounts”
 inbox

•Process vendor invoices accurately and timely in Intacct

•Post recurring bills and ensure proper coding

•Handle credit card transaction processing and reconciliation

•Perform W-9 verification via IRS website

•Prepare and post AP-related journal entries

•Support annual 1099 preparation and reporting



Expense Reporting (Brex)

•Set up and maintain Brex user accounts

•Review and validate Brex credit card charges

•Conduct weekly expense reimbursement reviews



Minimum Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field

2–4+ years of accounts payable or general accounting experience

Experience with ERP systems (Intacct preferred) and expense tools (e.g., Brex)

Strong understanding of AP processes, vendor management, and payment cycles

Familiarity with U.S. compliance requirements (W-9, 1099 reporting)

Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Microsoft Office/365

Ability to work U.S. hours (PST) with flexibility as needed



Key Attributes for Success



Strong attention to detail and accuracy in high-volume processing

Excellent organizational and time management skills

Ability to work independently and meet deadlines

Strong communication skills, both written and verbal

High level of integrity and professionalism

Collaborative mindset with the ability to work cross-functionally

Proactive problem-solving and process improvement mindset



Perks and Benefits

Professional Development: Ongoing training and exposure to U.S. accounting practices

Career Growth: Clear progression path within the accounting organization

Global Exposure: Work closely with U.S.-based teams and clients

Work Flexibility: Remote work environment with collaborative tools

title: Bookkeeper

title: Artist Needed for Simple Children's Coloring Books (Midjourney)
description:
Job Description:

I'm building and running an Etsy store focused on simple black and white coloring books for young children (ages 3–6). I'm looking for a reliable and talented artist to help create them.





What we need:

Very thick bold black outlines

Large, simple, and chunky shapes

Plenty of white space

Cute, friendly, rounded characters

Clean and easy-to-color designs for toddlers





Payment Structure:

$55 per completed 32-page book (paid after approval)

$1.00 commission per sale on every book you work on (paid monthly)



Artists who consistently complete 6–8 good books per month typically earn $450 – $700 per month, with the potential to earn $800 – $1,000+ as the store grows.

This is an ongoing role helping to build and expand the store. Ideal for someone who enjoys creating simple, high-quality coloring pages and can work independently.

If you have experience with Midjourney (or similar AI tools) and can deliver clean, thick-line toddler-style artwork, please apply with 3–5 examples of your simple children's coloring book pages.



Looking forward to seeing your work.

title: CapCut Video Editor
description:
** Please Read Entire Post Before Contacting **



Hello, currently in need of a video editor that has experience with CapCut and ElevenLabs (very easy to use). I have 20 faceless YouTube channels. I am looking to hire 2 assistants to split the workload or one assistant available that can handle the 20.



I will provide access to all paid accounts. The channels are audio based, visuals are not important. Do not source separate clips based on the content of the videos except for rare occasions. We will recycle the same 5-6 backgrounds and rotate them daily across all videos. Every video each day will have the same background across all channels, we simply change it each day.



You can simply save the long background clip separately once made in CapCut and keep it in a folder for future use as it cuts down editing time significantly.



The process would be as follows:



- I would provide the script, you paste the script and generate the audio in ElevenLabs, download the audio, then create a basic video in CapCut with subtitles for the audio.



- The backgrounds can be looped clips from Pexel or Envato, stretched to match the time of the audio (usually 10-20 minutes). 



- You download the video and upload to YouTube. I would provide the video titles, descriptions, tags, category, and the ChatGPT prompt to generate the thumbnail.



- You simply need to edit the videos so that it flows well and to generate the subtitles.



Must be knowledgeable in editing videos and must be able to know how to use CapCut. ElevenLabs is easy to use and I will provide a tutorial video going over the process to confirm the job.



Will be working about 3-5 hours a day as the videos only take about 10 minutes each to edit, I can show how in the tutorial videos.

title: Cinematic Video Editor - Events, Corporate & Storytelling
description:
About Us



We are a U.S.-based video production company focused on creating high-end cinematic videos for corporate clients, events, and brand storytelling. We create story-driven videos that combine interviews, music, and cinematic b-roll to deliver emotional and engaging content. While our focus is cinematic storytelling, occasional social media-style projects may be included based on client needs. We also do a great deal of photography work, mostly events and headshot style photography. 



IMPORTANT – Please Read Before Applying



We are NOT looking for: TikTok editors, YouTube vlog editors or fast, high-volume editing



We ARE looking for: A cinematic editor who understands storytelling, pacing, and emotion



Role Overview



You will be responsible for editing: Event highlight videos, cinematic intro videos, corporate storytelling videos, multi-cam full-length speaker event videos, templated projects and occasional photography editing projects. 



This is a growth opportunity. The position will start as a part time position, but there's opportunity to grow into a full-time role. 



These projects require:

Strong sense of story

Clean, professional editing

Ability to cut to music and build emotion



What You’ll Be Doing:

Edit cinematic videos (1–3 minutes), including event highlights and brand storytelling

Edit multi-cam speaker presentations (30–40 minutes)

Assist with templated projects and occasional photo editing

Combine interviews and b-roll into a cohesive story

Select and cut music appropriately

Maintain a clean, modern, professional editing style

Work closely with the creative director to bring high-quality storytelling projects to life.

Deliver first drafts for internal review



Requirements:

Strong understanding of cinematic storytelling

Ability to edit based on emotion, not just timing

Experience with event videos, weddings, or documentaries

Ability to use interviews to drive narrative

Strong attention to detail and ability to take feedback and improve quickly

Clean editing style (no overuse of effects)

Good English communication

Reliable and meets deadlines



MUST HAVE (Portfolio Requirements)

You must provide 2–3 examples of:

Cinematic videos

Event highlights OR wedding films OR brand storytelling



Applications without relevant portfolio will NOT be considered



Bonus Skills (Not Required)

Sound design

Color grading experience

Motion graphics editing

Photo editing - Lightroom

Experience with corporate clients



Paid Test Project



We will provide a short paid test project to selected candidates.



This helps us evaluate:



Your storytelling ability

Your pacing and editing decisions



Compensation

Starting at $8–$12/hour (based on experience)

This position starts as part-time (10–20 hours/week), with strong potential to grow into a full-time role

Consistent project flow for the right candidate

Performance-based pay increases over time

Bonus opportunities available after long-term engagement



How to Apply (IMPORTANT)



To apply, include:



Portfolio links (REQUIRED)

A short message answering this:



What makes a video feel “cinematic” to you?



Your favorite type of project to edit



Applications that do NOT follow instructions will be ignored

Include the word “Cinematic” at the top of your application so we know you read this.

title: SHORT FORM VIDEO EDITOR | Flexible Work Schedule
description:
We’re hiring someone who knows how to turn long-form content into sharp, high-retention short videos for platforms like TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts.



This role is for someone who understands pacing, hooks, and visual rhythm in short-form content, and knows how to edit with intention rather than just cutting clips.



—-----------------------------------------------------------------------------



READ THE ENTIRE JOB LISTING CAREFULLY. The final step of the hiring process will require the candidate to provide at least 1 former employer/team lead reference and at least 1 character reference. These will be verified prior to extending an offer.



--- ABOUT THE TEAM ---

Want to work in a rewarding, fast-paced, start-up atmosphere? Looking for significant growth and performance bonus opportunities? Have a growth mindset, value relationships, and dedicated to getting results? Then we might be a fit!



What is it like to work with us? HEAR FROM OUR TEAM!   





Our Core Values:

1) DELIGHT THE CLIENT:  We consistently exceed expectations by anticipating needs, delivering outstanding service, and creating those “magic 
nts” where every client feels seen and heard.

2) MASTER YOUR CRAFT:  We commit to continuous learning, share our expertise openly, and tackle every challenge with precision and a deep sense of duty.

3) FOLLOW THROUGH, ALWAYS:  When we say something will happen, it does—every time. We own every task, ensuring that nothing falls through the cracks, and when mistakes occur, we learn and improve immediately.



Our core values are important and the right candidate will share our values. We are building an awesome place to work, not just cutting together videos for our real estate business.



--- ABOUT YOU ---

Do you have strong creative instincts and know how to spot 
nts that make people stop scrolling? Are you proactive, adaptable, and detail-oriented? Do you have a sharp sense of pacing, an instinct for hooks, and a clear understanding of how short-form videos need to flow to hold attention? Do you take pride in clean edits and delivering videos that feel intentional and finished? Do our core values sound like a fit for who you are?



Then keep reading to see if we're a fit!



Responsibilities:

Short-Form Editing & Story Selection

- Review long-form videos, transcripts, or raw footage to identify short-form clip opportunities

- Select 
nts that are clear, engaging, and platform-appropriate

- Edit vertical videos optimized for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts

- Create strong hooks, clean cuts, and natural pacing

- Deliver publish-ready videos that meet brand and platform standards



Captions, Visuals & Formatting

- Add on-screen captions (burned-in or SRT as required) with proper timing and readability

- Apply basic motion, zooms, punch-ins, and visual emphasis where appropriate

- Follow platform-specific formatting (aspect ratio, duration, safe margins)



Iteration & Workflow

- Implement feedback efficiently and meet agreed turnaround times

- Maintain clear file naming and folder organization

- Track edits, revisions, and delivery status as required



Requirements:

- A STABLE, HIGH-SPEED INTERNET CONNECTION, REQUIRED (at least 150mbps)

- Experience with Final Cut Pro X (or other editing software, such as Adobe After Effects and Premiere Pro) is required

- Strong preference for MacOS users

- Experience editing short-form vertical content for social platforms, required

- Strong understanding of short-form pacing, hooks, and retention

- Experience working from transcripts or long-form content, required

- Clean file organization and understanding of post-production workflo

- Portfolio of completed short-form video projects, required

- General understanding of brand, content marketing, YouTube, etc, required

- GREAT interpersonal and communication skills, required

- Ability to self-start, work on a tight schedule, and manage multiple project deadlines, required

- Ability to follow directions and a LOVE of completing lots of projects according to consistent specifications, required

- Growth mindset, required



--- APPLICATION & RECRUITMENT PROCESS ---

STEP 1:

Fill out the application form through the link below and write "green kangaroo" in the Additional Comments field.


 



STEP 2:

Qualified candidates may be invited to participate in two PAID test projects, $10-20 USD for each test project estimated to be 2-3hrs of work for each (a total of $40 USD!).



STEP 3:

Candidates who advance through the test project rounds will be invited to an interview.



STEP 4:

Before extending a trial offer, we will conduct a CHARACTER REFERENCE CHECK for the final candidate(s). CONDUCTING A REFERENCE CHECK will be the last step in the hiring process.



STEP 5:

Upon receiving a trial offer, you will be onboarded as a Video Editor with a 90-day PAID trial period. During this period, we expect you to demonstrate your video editing expertise, meet project deadlines, be a team player, and excel at what you do. A successful trial will lead to a permanent offer to join the team.

title: Positioning & Launch Specialist
description:
Type of Work: Part-Time

Hours Per Week: 10

Compensation: $300/month to start

Location: Philippines



Job Overview



We are looking for a high-level marketer based in the Philippines to help us with the positioning, messaging, and overall launch strategy for a new product called the RoofFirst Kit, while also helping us align it with our roofing lead-generation business, Hampton Roofing Experts.



This is not a virtual assistant role.

This is not an account setup role.

This is not a general admin support role.



We already have support for account setup and execution tasks. What we need now is someone with real marketing experience who can help us think through the market, shape the offer, improve the messaging, and guide the launch process.



We are looking for someone who understands how to help bring a product to market the right way.



What You Will Help With



1. Product Positioning



Help us clearly position the RoofFirst Kit in the market



Refine the value proposition and core offer



Identify the strongest customer pain points, buying triggers, and product angles



Help differentiate the product from competitors and low-quality alternatives



Help us determine how to best connect the product to our service business





2. Launch Strategy



Help build the overall launch strategy from pre-launch through rollout



Advise on what should happen first, what should wait, and what is most important



Help prioritize the best launch channels and tactics



Help us identify what assets, messaging, and promotions are needed before launch



Help us avoid weak positioning and poor launch decisions





3. Messaging and Offer Development



Help refine the core marketing message for the product



Improve hooks, calls to action, landing page messaging, ad angles, and customer-facing copy



Help strengthen how we present the offer online



Assist with promotional ideas and conversion-focused messaging





4. Funnel and Conversion Strategy



Help us think through the customer journey from first impression to purchase or lead



Advise on how the websites, ads, and follow-up systems should work together



Help improve the conversion flow and launch readiness



Recommend what should be emphasized to build trust and increase action





5. Market and Competitive Insight



Help research competitors, similar products, and market opportunities



Help identify gaps in the market and positioning opportunities



Help evaluate where this product should be sold and how it should be introduced





Ideal Candidate



We are looking for someone who has real experience in some or most of the following:



product marketing



launch strategy



offer creation



direct response marketing



lead generation strategy



funnel strategy



conversion-focused messaging



e-commerce or product launches





You should be able to think strategically, not just complete tasks.



You should understand:



how to position a product



how to shape an offer



how to guide a launch



how marketing channels work together



how to improve messaging for conversion





We Are Not Looking For



a virtual assistant



a data entry person



a general admin support worker



a basic social media scheduler



someone whose main strength is setting up accounts or following checklists



someone whose experience is mostly clerical or assistant-based





Important Note



We already have support for setup and execution. We are specifically looking for someone who can bring marketing judgment, positioning insight, launch thinking, and strategic support to this project.



To Apply



Please answer all of the following:



1. What products, services, or brands have you helped launch?





2. What was your role in those launches?





3. Have you helped with product positioning, offer strategy, or launch planning before? Please explain.





4. What experience do you have improving messaging, conversion flow, or go-to-market strategy?





5. Share one example where your marketing input directly improved a launch, offer, or campaign.





6. Start your application with the words: RoofFirst Strategy







Main Goal



To help us position, present, and launch the RoofFirst Kit the right way so it has the strongest chance of success in the market.



If you want, I can also give you a shorter, punchier version that may perform even better on OnlineJobs.

title: Webflow Designer for Service-Based Websites
description:
Are you a Webflow designer who leans more editorial, refined, and minimal than techy and heavy?



We’re looking for a designer to support ongoing client projects.

Our work is strategy-led, clean, and conversion-focused, with a strong emphasis on white space, hierarchy, and restraint.



This is not a role for “just build it” developers.

We care about how things feel, not just how they function.



THE TYPE OF WORK WE DO



We design websites for service-based businesses.



Think

Clean layouts

Strong hierarchy

Generous white space

Refined typography

Subtle interactions

Elevated, considered design



Not

Dark, heavy, tech-style builds

Over-designed layouts

Cluttered sections or excessive effects



If your portfolio leans corporate, SaaS, or developer-heavy, this likely isn’t the right fit.



WHY YOU’LL ENJOY WORKING WITH US



Clear direction. Strategy, copy, and wireframes are provided

Defined scope. No guesswork, no vague briefs

Flexible hours. Work to deadlines, not fixed shifts

Respectful collaboration. No last-minute chaos

Long-term partnership. Consistent work for the right person



We prioritise quality over speed.



WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING



Build websites in Webflow based on provided strategy and wireframes

Translate brand direction into clean, high-end layouts

Structure CMS collections logically

Apply SEO basics during build

Refine and improve existing Webflow sites

Ensure all builds are easy for clients to manage



Optional



Design in Figma before build



REQUIRED SKILLS



WEBFLOW

Strong experience building in Webflow

Confident with CMS, components, and interactions

Clean builds only. No messy overrides or shortcuts



CLIENT-FIRST

Solid understanding of Client-First structure

Clear class naming and scalable systems



SEO BASICS

Proper heading structure

Meta titles and descriptions

Alt text and CMS SEO fields

Internal linking



THE NON-NEGOTIABLE



Your work must demonstrate

Strong use of white space

Clean, minimal layouts

High-end, service-based design

Thoughtful typography



We will not consider portfolios that



Feel overly masculine, tech-heavy, or corporate

Are cluttered or visually noisy

Prioritise effects over clarity



YOUR WORKING STYLE



You work well independently with clear direction

You communicate early if something’s off

You meet deadlines

You’re open to feedback

You take pride in clean, considered work



HOURS AND RATE



Part-time, ongoing

Flexible hours

Work to deadlines, not fixed shifts

Rate based on experience



HOW TO APPLY



2 to 4 live Webflow sites you personally built

At least one clean, service-based website

An example using Client-First

A short note on how you approach SEO in Webflow

Your hourly rate and weekly availability



Applications without relevant examples, do not reside in the Philippines or who do not provide the above will not be reviewed.

title: Customer Support Assistant (Remote)
description:
Customer Support Assistant (Remote) – Swedish E-Commerce Company



IMPORTANT

Salary for this position is 1 SEK per hour.

If you are not comfortable with this rate, please do not apply.



We are a Swedish company looking for a reliable and dedicated Customer Support Assistant to help us manage customer
s for several different brands.



We currently have a team of 11 people, and everyone is working well together. We are now looking to add one more person to the team.



This is a long-term position. If you do a great job, we want you to stay with us for a long time. There will also be opportunities to learn new tasks and grow into other roles in the company.



Most customer questions will be related to:



Order status (“Where is my delivery?”)

Orders not received

Returns and exchanges

General questions about the brand



You will work closely with our agents in China who handle packaging and shipping, as well as directly with our payment providers to resolve customer issues without needing our constant assistance.

Someone who is technical, consistent, hardworking, and always looking to improve

Experience with
 customer support and Shopify



Work Schedule



8 hours per day

5 days per week (option to work 6 or 7 days if you want more hours)

You can choose your working hours, but you must work the same schedule every day

Future Opportunities

Finding viral products

Searching for ad videos

Editing ad creatives

Writing ad copy

Building simple websites

Creating product descriptions

Communicating with UGC creators and influencers



Experience in these areas is a plus, but not required—we will train you to do them well.



Salary

How to Apply



Please send a message with the headline " CUSTOMER SUPPORT LONGTERM "



Please share:



A brief introduction about yourself

What you are currently doing

Your ambitions

Your relevant experience



We are looking for someone serious, long-term, and ready to grow with us.

title: Long-Term SEO Blog Writer for B2B Industrial Brands
description:
SEO Blog Writer Wanted (B2B / Industrial Focus)



We are a fast-growing digital marketing team based in China, working with **50+ international B2B manufacturers** across industrial sectors such as **valves, circuit breakers, packaging machinery, and electrical products**.



To support our long-term content strategy, we are looking for reliable freelance or part-time SEO blog writers to join our content team.



---



What We’re Looking For



**1. Strong English writing skills**

Native-level English or near-native proficiency, with clear structure, accurate grammar, and a professional B2B tone.



**2. Solid understanding of SEO content**

You should be familiar with writing **SEO-driven product category pages and blogs**, targeting keywords like *“industrial valve manufacturer”*, *“custom circuit breaker”*, and similar B2B search terms.



**3. Long-term, stable collaboration**

We prefer writers who can work with us consistently and deliver **at least one high-quality article (1,500–2,000 words) per week**.



**4. Good research & learning ability**

Many topics involve industrial or technical products. You don’t need prior industry experience, but you must be able to **quickly understand product features and translate them into buyer-oriented content**.





 How to Apply



Please prepare the following:



* Links to your **best B2B or SEO writing samples**

* A brief introduction to your **experience with SEO blog writing**



If you meet the above requirements, please complete our writer application form:

????

title: Credit Repair Specialist
description:
Part-Time Credit Repair Specialist & Mentor



I’m looking for an experienced Credit Repair Specialist to work with me part-time, helping not only with drafting and managing dispute letters but also teaching me the ins and outs of successful credit repair. I’d like someone who can guide me through the entire process — from creating effective dispute strategies and letters to freezing accounts, staying compliant, and keeping up with current credit repair methods.



This role is as much about mentorship as it is about doing the work. I already have access to Dispute Fox and some sample clients to work on, so you’ll have tools and examples ready to go. My goal is to dedicate about 2–4 hours a week to this process, learning hands-on while building real results together.



The schedule is flexible, the salary is negotiable, and I have no issue with you working elsewhere during our time together. If you’re knowledgeable in credit repair, confident in your dispute drafting skills, and enjoy teaching others how to succeed in this field, I’d love to hear from you.

title: Project / Contracts Administrator (Construction | Remote - AU)
description:
About the Role

We are looking for a highly organised and detail-driven Project / Contracts Administrator to support residential and remedial construction projects in Australia.



This role focuses on contract administration, procurement, cost tracking, and keeping project data accurate across multiple jobs. You will work closely with project managers to keep projects moving, on budget, and compliant.



Key Responsibilities



* Manage contracts including head contracts, subcontracts, variations and progress claims

* Prepare and issue RFQs and purchase orders, and track procurement schedules

* Assist with cost tracking, budget updates and cost-to-complete reporting

* Review and process subcontractor and supplier invoices against budgets

* Maintain Buildertrend (or similar) with up-to-date project data, selections and variations

* Coordinate approvals, shop drawings and consultant documentation

* Ensure compliance with NSW building requirements, insurances and contract obligations

* Support project managers to keep timelines, budgets and deliverables on track





What We’re Looking For



* Experience in construction administration (residential or remedial preferred)

* Strong understanding of contracts, variations and progress claims

* High attention to detail and strong organisation skills

* Ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines

* Confident using Buildertrend, Xero and Excel

* Strong written and verbal communication

* Proactive and able to work independently



Desirable



* Experience with NSW contracts (HIA, MBA, cost-plus)

* Understanding of local compliance (NCC, DA, CDC, CC, OC processes)



Why Join



* Long-term remote role with an Australian construction team

* Stable workload across multiple projects

* Clear systems and processes in place

* Opportunity to grow into a senior project admin role



To Apply

Send your CV, a short summary of your experience, and examples of projects or systems you’ve worked with.

title: Amazon FBA Operations Manager – (High-Volume OA/Wholesale Support)
description:
I am an active Amazon seller currently moving between 800–1,200 units per month through Online Arbitrage and Wholesale. As the business scales, the logistics, sourcing coordination, and "profit leaks" are becoming too much to handle alone.



I am looking for a Right-Hand Operations Manager who actually understands the FBA ecosystem. I don't need a data entry clerk; I need someone who can look at my spreadsheets, identify where I'm losing money (tax, shipping, prep fees), and help me optimize the entire supply chain.



Your Role:



Cost Optimization: Identifying "Profit Leaks" in the sourcing process and finding ways to recover margin.



Sourcing Research: Finding high-volume, high-margin leads that fit our current model.



Growth Support: Assisting with high-level outreach to distributors and brands.



Skill Requirements

Amazon Expert: Must be proficient in Seller Central and FBA workflows.



Spreadsheet Ninja: Advanced Google Sheets/Excel skills for tracking unit costs and margins especially with SALES TAX states



Problem Solver: You must be able to explain one way you’ve saved a previous Amazon client money on their operations.



English Fluency: Professional written communication for US-based vendor outreach.



Tools: Experience with SellerAMP, Keepa, or Helium10 is a plus.

title: Shopify Store Operations Specialist
description:
Shopify Store Operations Specialist

Location: Remote (Philippines-based)

Type: Full-Time

Level: Mid–Senior

Daily Rate: ?2,000 – ?2,500



About the Role

Hair Doctors is a fast-growing Australian haircare brand preparing to launch our e-commerce store. We are building our Shopify store now and we need a detail-oriented operations specialist to keep it running cleanly, accurately, and efficiently from day one.

This is not a marketing role. This is not a development role. This is the person who makes sure the store works — products are correct, orders are processed, the app stack is maintained, and the data is clean. If you take pride in operational excellence and you know Shopify inside out, this role is for you.

You will be the engine room of our store. The marketing team depends on you for clean product data. The CRM team depends on you for accurate customer and order data. The business depends on you for a store that runs without problems.



Core Accountabilities

Product & Catalogue Management



Upload and maintain all product listings — titles, descriptions, images, variants, pricing, and tags — across our two ranges and multiple SKUs

Build and manage collections, ensuring products are correctly categorised and displayed

Maintain metafields for dynamic content requirements

Keep product data accurate and consistent at all times — no outdated pricing, no broken images, no missing variants

Coordinate with the design and content team to ensure product content meets brand standards before going live



Order Management & Fulfilment



Process and manage orders from placement through to fulfilment

Handle order exceptions — refunds, cancellations, address corrections, and customer queries escalated from support

Monitor fulfilment status and flag delays or issues proactively

Manage B2B draft orders and wholesale customer accounts

Coordinate with fulfilment partners or logistics providers as required



App Stack & Store Health



Install, configure, and maintain third-party Shopify apps

Conduct regular audits of the app stack — identify redundancies, unused apps, and anything that may be slowing the store down

Monitor store health proactively — page speed, broken links, checkout errors, and pixel firing

Escalate technical issues that require developer intervention and follow through until resolved

Ensure all apps are correctly integrated and not conflicting with each other or the theme



Customer Data & Shopify CRM



Maintain Shopify customer records — tags, segments, purchase history, and custom properties

Ensure customer data is clean, correctly tagged, and accurately reflects purchase behaviour

Support the Klaviyo CRM specialist by ensuring Shopify events are firing correctly and customer data is structured consistently

Set up and manage customer account settings and B2B company profiles



Promotions & Merchandising



Set up and manage discount codes, promotional offers, and gift card programmes

Configure automatic discounts and ensure they are correctly scoped and tested before going live

Support product launches by ensuring new products are correctly listed, linked, and visible at the right time

Assist with basic visual merchandising — product ordering within collections, featured products, and homepage content updates



Reporting



Provide weekly operational reporting covering store performance, order volumes, and any issues identified

Monitor Shopify analytics and flag anything unusual — traffic drops, conversion dips, checkout abandonment spikes

Track inventory levels and alert the team to low stock before it becomes a problem





Who You Are



Has managed a Shopify store day-to-day for a DTC or eCommerce brand — not just set one up, but run one operationally for a sustained period

Genuinely detail-oriented — you notice when a product image is wrong, a price is outdated, or a tag is missing

Proactive — you flag problems before they are pointed out to you

Comfortable working independently and managing your own priorities without constant direction

Strong written English — you will be communicating with an Australian team across different time zones, primarily async

3–5 years of hands-on Shopify experience at a mid-to-senior operational level

Experience working with foreign clients, preferably Australian, US, or UK





Platform Skills (Non-Negotiable)

Shopify Admin



Fully proficient in Shopify admin — products, collections, orders, customers, discounts, apps, and analytics

Understands Shopify metafields and how to use them for dynamic content

Can manage theme customisations using the Theme Editor without developer support

Understands Shopify's customer segmentation and tagging system

Familiar with Shopify markets and multi-currency settings



Shopify Events & Tracking



Understands how Shopify events work — viewed product, added to cart, started checkout, placed order, fulfilled order

Can validate that pixels and tracking scripts are firing correctly

Can identify when events are misfiring and escalate with the right information for a developer to fix



App Ecosystem



Experience managing a multi-app Shopify stack

Familiarity with common DTC app categories — reviews, upsell, loyalty, subscription, wholesale, page builder

Can assess whether an app is causing performance issues and recommend alternatives



Reporting & Analytics



Can navigate Shopify analytics and reports confidently

Understands key ecommerce metrics — conversion rate, AOV, returning customer rate, cart abandonment





Bonus (Not Required)



Experience with PageFly or similar Shopify page builders

Experience with wholesale or B2B order management

Familiarity with Klaviyo or Omnisend and how Shopify data feeds into them

Experience with inventory management apps





How to Apply

Applications without demonstrated hands-on Shopify operations experience will not be progressed.

Please include:



A specific example of a Shopify store you have managed operationally — what you were responsible for, how many SKUs, and what the day-to-day looked like

How you have handled a store problem or error that you identified proactively — what it was, how you found it, and what you did

The Shopify apps you have the most experience with and what you used them for

Your desired daily rate in PHP and your availability

title: AI Content Creator – Carousels & Video
description:
We're looking for a creative, AI-native content producer who lives and breathes short-form content. You'll use cutting-edge AI tools to generate carousels and vertical videos that are scroll-stopping, on-brand, and built to drive views, engagement, and shares.

This isn't a "learn on the job" role although keeping up to speed with updates will be a requirement,  you need to already be obsessed with AI content tools and deeply fluent in what makes content pop on social.



Use AI tools (provided) to produce high-volume carousels and vertical video content

Write and refine creative prompts to generate visuals, animations, and video that feel fresh and on-brand

Maintain strict brand consistency across all output — colors, tone, style, energy

Stay ahead of platform trends on TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, and

Iterate fast based on performance data and feedback





What You Need



Genuine passion for AI-generated content — you're already experimenting with these tools on your own

Deep understanding of creative prompting (image, video, and motion-based AI tools)

Sharp eye for brand consistency and visual quality

Strong instinct for what performs — you know the difference between content that gets skipped and content that gets shared

Fluency in short-form platform culture (trends, formats, pacing, hooks)

title: Trailer Management Assistant
description:
GH Logistics is a high performance freight brokerage and asset based logistics company specializing in dry van and refrigerated freight. We move quickly, but we move with structure. Our operation is built on real-time visibility, disciplined execution, and clear communication. We take pride in keeping freight organized, assets accounted for, and operations running cleanly from pickup to delivery.



If you’re someone who enjoys structured execution, asset visibility, and maintaining operational accuracy behind the scenes, you’ll feel right at home here.



Role Overview

You support the Trailer Manager in overseeing a fleet of 250+ trailers across multiple carriers and terminals. You play a critical role in ensuring every trailer is accounted for, properly assigned, compliant, and operational. This role is built for someone who thrives on organization, precision, and maintaining consistency across a fast-moving logistics environment.



What You’ll Be Doing

• Monitor, update, and reconcile trailer locations across the network on a daily basis

• Coordinate with dispatchers, drivers, and terminals to ensure accurate trailer assignments and timely movement

• Track trailer availability, maintenance schedules, and repair status

• Maintain accurate and up-to-date trailer documentation (registrations, inspections, tags, insurance)

• Support reporting on trailer utilization, downtime, and compliance

• Identify discrepancies, missing updates, or unreported trailer statuses and escalate promptly

• Assist in improving internal trailer tracking systems for better visibility and accuracy

• Execute processes consistently with strong attention to detail

• Collaborate cross-functionally with dispatch, compliance, and maintenance teams



What Success Looks Like

• Trailer locations and statuses are consistently accurate and fully reconciled

• Assignments, availability, and movements are properly documented and updated in real time

• Maintenance and compliance records are complete and up to date

• Discrepancies are identified early and resolved before impacting operations

• Reporting on utilization and downtime is clean, accurate, and actionable

• Communication across departments is clear, timely, and structured



Who We’re Looking For

• Experience in fleet management, logistics coordination, dispatch, or similar operational roles (preferred)

• Strong attention to detail with high accuracy in tracking and record-keeping

• Organized and capable of managing multiple data points across systems and teams

• Clear and professional communication skills across departments

• Familiarity with platforms such as Samsara, LoadStop, or similar tracking systems (a plus)

• Proactive mindset, able to identify and address issues before they escalate

• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills in a fast-paced environment

• Minimum 45 WPM typing speed

• Proficient in Microsoft Office and Google Workspace

• Reliable internet connection and fully equipped remote work setup

• Team oriented, coachable, and aligned with structured processes



Why Join GH Logistics?

• Structured systems and clear operational expectations

• Hands on leadership with direct feedback

• Defined KPIs and measurable performance growth

• Opportunity to grow within a scaling logistics operation

• Collaborative, team oriented environment

• Recognition based on performance and contribution

• Permanent work from home setup

• Paid training upon successful onboarding



Schedule & Work Setup

• Full-Time Remote Role | Direct Hire to a U.S. Company

• Possible Shifts: 7 AM – 5 PM CDT | 3 PM - 1 AM CDT | 11 PM - 9 AM CDT (San Antonio time)

• Zoom presence required (camera on, audio unmuted)

• Time-tracking software (Hubstaff) used throughout the shift



This role is built for someone who takes pride in maintaining control over moving assets, ensuring trailers are accounted for, properly assigned, and always operational. If you value structure, accuracy, and keeping operations running clean behind the scenes, you’ll thrive in this role.



Apply Here:





Visit Us:

title: INSURANCE AGENCY TECH AUTOMATION & SYSTEMS MANAGER
description:
Insurance Tech Automation Manager – Philippines

$8-$11/hour | Full-Time | Philippines Day Shift

Weer Insurance Group (Chicago, IL) is hiring a full-time Tech Automation & Systems Manager to own our tech stack and eliminate technical bottlenecks.



The Role

Company: Weer Insurance Group – Independent commercial insurance agency (contractors, trucking, restaurants)

Schedule: Monday–Friday, 8 AM - 5 PM Philippines time (6 PM - 3 AM US Central, previous day)

Work Style: Async-first with weekly live meeting. Work gets done while you sleep, you review results each morning.

Pay: $8-11/hour based on experience

Type: Full-time, 40 hours/week

Growth: Clear path to $11-$13/hour within 12-18 months



What You'll Do

Your Mission: The CEO spends zero time troubleshooting tech issues. Automations run flawlessly. Leads never get lost.

-Automation & Systems (40%): Build and monitor Zapier workflows, optimize Agency Zoom/EZLynx integrations, eliminate manual data entry

-Lead Tracking & Analytics (25%): Track lead sources, build conversion dashboards, monitor ROI by marketing channel


 Marketing (15%): Manage renewal campaigns, monthly newsletters, A/B testing, deliverability optimization

-Social Media (15%): Create 2-3 posts/week using Canva + SocialPilot, maintain content calendar, track engagement

-Proactive Problem-Solving (5%): Spot inefficiencies and fix them before being asked

Success in 90 Days: CEO troubleshooting time = 0 hours. You're suggesting system improvements he hasn't thought of yet.



Who We Want

Required Experience:

-3+ years automation/CRM experience (Zapier, Make, API integrations)

-GoHighLevel, HubSpot, Salesforce, or similar marketing automation platforms


 marketing & deliverability (SPF/DKIM/DMARC, campaign management)

-Social media management (content creation, scheduling, analytics)

-Strong written English and proactive problem-solving mindset

-Reliable infrastructure: 10+ Mbps internet, backup connection, UPS

Highly Preferred (Not Required):

-US insurance agency experience (Agency Zoom, EZLynx, AMS360, Applied Epic)

-GoHighLevel certification or advanced automation credentials

-Experience with lead tracking and marketing analytics tools

-API integration and webhook experience

Note: Insurance experience is a bonus, not a requirement. We value automation expertise and the ability to learn quickly over industry background.



Compensation & Benefits

Starting Rate (Based on Experience):

Experience Level	Starting	After 90 Days	Year 1

Strong automation, no insurance	$8/hour	$9/hour	$10/hour

Automation + insurance experience	$9/hour	$10/hour	$11/hour

Senior expert + certifications	$10/hour	$11/hour	$12/hour



Long-Term Growth Path:

-Year 2-3: $11-$13/hour (automation team lead)

-Year 4-5: $13-$15/hour (managing 2-3 VAs, strategic systems oversight)

Benefits:

-13th month bonus

-10 days PTO

-5 sick days

-Philippine holidays paid

-Payment via Wise (bi-weekly)



Why This Role Stands Out

-Philippines daytime schedule – Work normal waking hours (8 AM - 5 PM PHT), not graveyard shift. Healthy, sustainable long-term.

-Async-first workflow – Work independently while CEO sleeps, deliver results he reviews each morning. Weekly live meeting for sync.

-Direct CEO relationship – You're a strategic partner, not ticket #4,729 at an outsourcing firm

-Real ownership – Manage the entire tech stack, suggest improvements, implement solutions

-Long-term career path – 3-5+ year opportunity (sustainable schedule = lower burnout, higher retention)

-Competitive compensation – Fair market rate for day shift, clear growth path to $11-13/hour





How to Apply

: 

Subject: Tech Automation – [Your Name] – [Years Experience] – [Automation Platform]

Include:

1. Portfolio (Required)

3-5 automation workflows you've built with:

-Platform used (Zapier, GoHighLevel, Make, etc.)

-Business problem solved

-Measurable results (time saved, leads captured, revenue impact)

2. Answer These 5 Screening Questions:

Q1: A Zap connecting your CRM to Google Sheets stops working. What are your first 5 troubleshooting steps?

Q2: Website lead volume drops 60% in one week. What are 3 possible causes and how do you diagnose each?

Q3:
 open rate is 12% vs. 18% benchmark. What 3 things would you test to improve it?

Q4: Write a social media post for a commercial insurance agency serving contractors. Include copy + graphic description (Canva).

Q5: Producers spend 3 hours/week manually checking policy renewals. How would you automate this workflow?

3. One Case Study (
words)

-Problem: What challenge did the business face?

-Solution: What automation/system did you build?

-Results: Measurable outcomes (time saved, revenue increased, error reduction)

4. Infrastructure Confirmation

-Internet speed + backup plan

-Power backup (UPS/generator)

-Availability to start

5. References

2 professional contacts (automation clients, managers, or collaborators)



Interview Process

Timeline: 2-3 weeks from application to offer

1.	Application Review (3-5 days)

2.	Technical Interview (30 min) – Dive into your portfolio, automation thinking, problem-solving

3.	Paid Test Project ($100, 8-10 hours) – Design an automation workflow for a real insurance scenario

4.	Reference Check (1-2 days)

5.	Offer



What Makes a Strong Candidate

-Technical depth: You can explain why an automation works, not just how to build it

-Proactive problem-solving: You spot issues before they become problems

-Clear communication: You document workflows so others can maintain them

-Business thinking: You measure impact in time saved and revenue generated, not features shipped

-Growth mindset: You're comfortable learning insurance-specific workflows (we'll teach you)



Weer Insurance Group | Chicago, Illinois

Equal Opportunity Employer

We value automation expertise over insurance experience.

If you're a strong technical problem-solver who can learn quickly, we want to hear from you.

Exposure to a wide range of industries and projects

Opportunity to grow into a full-time role

Work that directly impacts client growth and performance

title: WordPress / Divi VA Needed — Divi-First Developer
description:
Divi 4 required, Divi 5 a strong plus



I’m looking for a WordPress VA / web developer who is genuinely strong in Divi and can help build, update, improve, and maintain websites across multiple brands and client projects.



My main need right now is WordPress + Divi, especially on sites that may also use WooCommerce. I’m also moving older sites into Divi 5 and starting new projects in Divi 5, so I need someone who truly understands Divi and works efficiently inside it.



Important



I use Divi as my main builder.



Because of that, I am not looking for someone whose main experience is Elementor and who only occasionally works in Divi. Elementor may be great, but this role is for someone who is truly Divi-first.



If your portfolio mainly shows Elementor sites and Divi is secondary, this is probably not the right fit.



What I need help with



I’m looking for someone who can:



build pages and full websites in WordPress + Divi

work on Divi + WooCommerce sites

improve existing websites

follow an existing design system using:

variables

presets

clean layout structure

responsive design best practices

make updates, tweaks, page builds, and ongoing edits

work cleanly inside the builder without creating a mess behind the scenes

help with ongoing client site support

communicate clearly when working on client-related requests

possibly assist with support or ticket-based workflows when needed



If the fit is good, this can become an ongoing long-term role.



Must-have experience



To apply, you must have:



strong hands-on experience with WordPress

strong hands-on experience with Divi

real Divi 4 experience

ability to show live websites you personally built using Divi

experience with WooCommerce

strong understanding of:

mobile responsiveness

spacing and typography

clean page structure

basic SEO best practices

site speed / performance basics

conversion-focused layouts

strong communication and attention to detail

Strong bonus experience



These are not required, but are a big plus:



experience with Divi 5

ability to show sites built in Divi 5

experience with Shopify

experience connecting stores to
, Instagram, and TikTok shops

experience with Rank Math

experience with WP All Import

experience with WPForms

experience with Google Drive integrations

experience working inside cPanel

experience with Go High Level

experience with WHMCS

experience handling client requests through a support or ticket system

ability to help with simple graphics or web-ready visual assets

What I do not want



Please do not apply if:



Elementor is your main builder and Divi is secondary

you cannot show real Divi-built examples

you rely heavily on custom code for things that should be built natively in Divi

you build slowly on standard page tasks

your builder structure is messy, overcomplicated, or inconsistent

How I like websites built



I want websites built with:



clean, modern design

strong mobile responsiveness

clear layout hierarchy

good user experience

good basic SEO setup

consistency across the site

efficient, organized builder structure



If you work in Divi, I want someone who knows how to build cleanly and efficiently, not just someone who can make a page look finished on the front end.



Divi-specific note



I’m especially interested in someone who:



has real experience building in Divi 4

understands how Divi projects should be structured

is comfortable moving into or already working in Divi 5

can show actual examples of Divi work, not just say they know it



If you already have real Divi 5 experience, that is a major plus.



Type of work involved



Projects may include:



standard content pages

marketing pages

WooCommerce product, category, and store pages

landing pages

support/help pages

ongoing edits for my brands

client site updates and maintenance



Some work may also include:



social commerce linking

plugin-based workflows

SEO setup

forms/imports/integrations

client communication-related tasks

End your reply to me by saying Divi-First so i know you saw this. 

support or ticket-based follow-up



To apply, please send all of the following



1. Divi examples



Send 3–5 live websites you personally built using Divi.



For each site, tell me:



whether it was built in Divi 4 or Divi 5

what parts you personally handled

whether it included WooCommerce

whether you handled mobile optimization, SEO setup, or performance improvements



2. Your Divi experience



Tell me:



how long you have been working with Divi

how much real experience you have with Divi 4

whether you have worked in Divi 5

whether you can show any real Divi 5 examples



3. Your WordPress / WooCommerce experience



Tell me your experience with:



WordPress

WooCommerce

Rank Math

WP All Import

WPForms

Google Drive integrations

cPanel

 / Instagram / TikTok shop linking

Go High Level

WHMCS

Shopify

client support or ticket systems



4. Your speed



Please tell me, on average, how long it takes you to complete:



a simple content/info page in Divi

a more polished marketing page in Divi

a WooCommerce-related or more involved page



Also tell me:



how you estimate time before starting

how you keep page builds efficient and on track



5. Your availability



Please include:



your hourly rate

how many hours per week you are available

your time zone

Important application instruction



Start your application with:



Divi-first developer



That helps me know you actually read the full post.



Final note



I’m looking for someone who understands the difference between:



just knowing WordPress

and being truly efficient in Divi



If you are organized, responsive, efficient, and can show real Divi work, I’d love to hear from you.

title: Appointment setter/closer/trial activation
description:
Title: Trial Activation Specialist (US Market, SaaS Automotive)



Apply via Google form





We’re hiring a full-time Trial Activation Specialist to intercept new trial users in the first minutes after signup, diagnose their setup, improve their configuration, and qualify serious operators for a founder call.



This is not a traditional sales role.

This is not appointment setting.

This is not customer support.



APPLICATION VIA GOOGLE FORM BELOW!



Your job is to:



Contact new trial users immediately after signup



Understand how they currently source vehicles



Diagnose whether their Swoopa setup is correctly configured



Apply one or two tactical improvements live on the call



Identify serious operators vs casual testers



Book qualified operators into a follow-up call with the founder



Follow structured scripts and qualification criteria



Keep clean notes and tagging in the CRM



Participate in call reviews and process improvement





You are not expected to hard-close.

You are expected to think clearly, guide confidently, and qualify accurately. 





---



What You’ll Do



Call new trial users within minutes of signup



Ask structured diagnostic questions about their sourcing workflow



Guide users through quick setup improvements



Recognize high-intent operators using defined criteria



Book qualified users into a founder strategy call



Send brief SMS/email follow-ups when required



Log outcomes cleanly and consistently



Follow and improve scripts based on performance data







---



Requirements



Fluent or near-native American accent



Experience speaking with US customers



Prior SDR, appointment-setting, or closing experience (minimum 1–2 years)



Able to follow structured scripts without freelancing the pitch



Comfortable guiding users through simple technical steps



Calm, clear, non-pushy phone presence



Strong written English for short follow-ups



Reliable internet, quiet workspace, proper headset (important)



Able to work US business hours



Full-time, long-term availability





MALE ONLY applicants ? we are working in a male dominant niche. 





---



Performance Metrics



% of new trials contacted within 5 minutes of search creation. 



% of contacted users receiving a setup improvement



% of qualified operators booked to founder call



Downstream paid conversion rate of booked users





Bonuses are paid on qualified bookings and downstream conversions, not raw call volume.





---



Compensation



Base Salary: $1,500 USD / month



Performance Bonuses:



Bonus per qualified operator booking



Bonus per converted operator after founder call



Additional win-back bonus for reactivated past subscribers





Expected Earnings:



Solid performer: $1800–$2500/month



High performer: $2500–$3000/month







---



Application



Applications must be submitted via the form.

title: Tech Automation & Systems Manager — Insurance Agency (Chicago, IL)
description:
**$9–$11/hour | Full-Time 40 hrs/week | Philippines Day Shift | 1099 Contractor**



---



**THE COMPANY**



Weer Insurance Group is an independent commercial insurance agency based in Chicago, IL. We serve contractors, trucking companies, restaurants, and businesses across the Chicagoland metro and Wisconsin markets — approximately 4,400 active clients. You will work directly with the CEO, not a manager at an outsourcing firm.



---



**THE SCHEDULE**



Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM Philippines Time (PHT / GMT+8)

This is a Philippines DAY SHIFT. You work normal waking hours — no graveyard shift.



Work style: Async-first. You work independently while the CEO sleeps and deliver documented results he reviews each morning. Weekly live video check-in required.



---



**IMPORTANT — READ BEFORE APPLYING**



This role requires exactly 40 hours per week tracked via Hubstaff with task-level logging. Hours are non-negotiable. If you are looking for flexible or part-time work, this is not the right fit.



Daily EOD summary required every day — completed work, blockers, and next day's plan with specific counts and dates. "In progress" is not an acceptable status update.



After any system change you made, you are responsible for verifying all connected systems still work before closing the task. Attention to detail is not optional in this role.



---



**OUR TECH STACK — YOU WILL WORK IN THESE SYSTEMS FROM DAY ONE**



Agency Management: EZLynx (AMS), AgencyZoom (CRM + pipeline automation)

Automation: Zapier, Cognito Forms, Webhooks

 & DNS: Microsoft 365, GoDaddy, SPF/DKIM/DMARC, SendGrid (EZLynx infrastructure), MXToolbox

Social Media: SocialPilot, Canva

Communication: Microsoft Teams, LightSpeed VOIP

Time Tracking: Hubstaff (mandatory)

Document Automation: Adapt (carrier notices + non-payment workflows)

APIs: EZLynx open API (custom integration work)

Other: Tarmika (Commercial Insurnace Rater), Chrome Extension development (queued project)



---



**WHAT YOU WILL OWN**



Your mission: The CEO spends zero time troubleshooting tech issues. Automations run flawlessly. Leads never get lost.



Automation & Systems (40%)

— Build, test, and monitor Zapier workflows connecting EZLynx, AgencyZoom, and Cognito Forms

— Migrate and document automation logic so it can be maintained by others

— Eliminate manual data entry bottlenecks

— Verify all connected systems still function after every change you make



Pipeline & CRM Management (25%)

— Build and activate AgencyZoom pipeline automations for Commercial Lines and Personal Lines

— Manage lead list scrubbing, segmentation, and pipeline loading

— Track lead counts, pipeline movement, and automation errors daily



 Deliverability & Campaigns (15%)

— Own SPF, DKIM, and DMARC configuration across all agency domains

— Monitor and optimize AgencyZoom
 sequences — report open rates on a consistent schedule

— A/B test subject lines; diagnose and fix deliverability failures



Social Media (15%)

— Schedule 2–3 posts per week via SocialPilot 
, LinkedIn, Instagram)

— Create graphics in Canva; maintain content calendar; track engagement monthly



Proactive Problem-Solving (5%)

— Spot inefficiencies and surface them with a proposed solution, not just a problem statement

— Flag blockers the same day they are discovered



---



**REQUIRED EXPERIENCE**



— 3+ years automation/CRM experience — Zapier, Make, or equivalent

— API integration and webhook experience — you can build custom connections, not just pre-built Zaps

—
 deliverability — you understand SPF/DKIM/DMARC and can diagnose why
s land in spam

— DNS management — you know what an A record, CNAME, and TXT record are and how they interact

— Strong written English — your daily summaries and client-facing automations must be professional

— Reliable infrastructure: 10+ Mbps internet, backup connection, UPS power backup



Preferred (not required):

— US insurance agency experience — EZLynx, AgencyZoom, AMS360, or Applied Epic

— Chrome Extension development

— GoHighLevel, HubSpot, or Salesforce automation experience



---



**COMPENSATION**



This is a 1099 independent contractor position. No benefits. Pay via Wise, bi-weekly.



Starting rate based on experience:

— Strong automation, no insurance experience: $9/hour ? $10 after 90 days ? $11 Year 1

— Automation + insurance agency experience: $10/hour ? $11 after 90 days ? $12 Year 1

— Senior expert + API/certifications: $11/hour ? $12 after 90 days ? $13 Year 1



Long-term path: $11–$13/hour Year 2–3 (automation team lead) | $13–$15/hour Year 4–5



Rate increases are tied to consistent 40-hour weeks, on-time deliverables, and measurable output — not tenure alone.



---



**HOW TO APPLY**



: 
 

Subject line: Tech Automation – [Your Name] – [Years Experience] – [Primary Platform]



Include all of the following:



1. PORTFOLIO (required)

3–5 automation workflows you have built. For each include: platform used, business problem solved, and measurable results (time saved, leads captured, error reduction).



2. ANSWER THESE 5 QUESTIONS



Q1: You update DNS records to fix an
 deliverability issue and the agency website goes down. What happened and what is your step-by-step recovery process?



Q2: An AgencyZoom
 sequence has a 3% open rate on
 1 and 0% on
s 2–5. Walk through your diagnosis and the first three changes you would make.



Q3: A Zapier workflow routing leads from a web form into a CRM stops firing. What are your first five troubleshooting steps?



Q4: The CEO asks you to automate follow-up for commercial prospects who haven't responded in 30 days. Describe the workflow you would build and how you would test it before activating on live leads.



Q5: Write a social media post for a commercial insurance agency targeting contractors in Illinois. Include post copy and a brief description of the Canva graphic you would pair with it.



3. ONE CASE STUDY (300–500 words)

Problem | Solution | Measurable Results



4. INFRASTRUCTURE CONFIRMATION

Internet speed and backup plan | Power backup (UPS or generator) | Available start date



5. TWO REFERENCES

Automation clients, managers, or direct collaborators



---



**INTERVIEW PROCESS (2–3 weeks)**



Step 1: Application review — portfolio and screening questions evaluated

Step 2: Technical interview (30 min) — live walkthrough of your portfolio

Step 3: Paid test project ($100, 8–10 hours) — real insurance automation scenario

Step 4: Reference check

Step 5: Offer



---



**WHAT MAKES A STRONG CANDIDATE**



— You can explain why an automation works, not just how to click through it

— You test everything connected to what you touched before you close a task

— You surface blockers the same day, not the next morning

— You measure your work in time saved and revenue impact, not features shipped

— You show up for 40 hours every week and your daily summaries prove it



---



Weer Insurance Group | Chicago, Illinois | 

Independent Contractor | We value automation expertise over insurance experience

title: Social Media Manager (Content Operator – Ownership Role)
description:
I’m looking for someone to own and operate my social media presence, not assist with it.



This is not a task-based VA role.

This is a performance-based execution role.



You will take ideas, voice notes, transcripts, and raw content and turn them into:



Structured content

Consistent posting

A streamlined content system



???? IMPORTANT – READ BEFORE APPLYING



What You’ll Be Responsible For:

Managing a 2-week rolling content calendar at all times

Creating and scheduling:

Reels

Trends

Trending audios

Carousels

Captions

Hashtags



Turning raw ideas into finished content independently

Maintaining brand voice and messaging consistency

Organizing content (Google Drive, Canva, Content Calendar, etc.)

Ensuring content is posted on time without reminders

Proactively improving and optimizing content



What This Role Is NOT:

You will not wait on me daily for direction

You will not operate week-to-week

You will not rely on motivation or “when you have time”



This role requires ownership, structure, and consistency



Requirements:

Proven experience managing (not assisting with) social media accounts

Strong reel + caption creation skills

Ability to take initiative and execute without hand-holding

Highly organized and system-oriented

Strong communication and follow-through



Non-Negotiables:

Content is always at least 1 week ahead (prefer 2-3 Weeks ahead for review and approvals)

Deadlines are met consistently

You take ownership of outcomes, not just tasks



???? APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS



Follow these EXACT steps:



1. Subject Line



When you apply, your subject line must be:



“SM Operator, not assistant – [Your Name]”



2. Loom Video (2–3 minutes)



Include a Loom link answering:



How do you plan and stay ahead on content weekly?

What would your first 14 days in this role look like?

How do you handle a client who is slow to respond?



3. Portfolio



Include 2–3 accounts you have fully managed

(Briefly explain what YOU were responsible for)



4. Attention to Detail Check



In your application, include this sentence at the very end:



“I read the full job description and follow instructions at a high level.”



5. Mini Thinking Test



In 2–3 sentences, answer this:



If I give you a 60-second talking video, how would you turn that into multiple pieces of content?

title: Lawyer / Legal Policy Specialist (Canada – Ontario Focus)
description:
